proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_palbociclib.
Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_tamoxifen.
Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_trastuzumab.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.408 in palbociclib-treated vs 4.310 in untreated (diff +1.098 months, Welch t-test p<1e-300). Strongly supported.
Mean pfs_months 4.723 in tamoxifen-treated vs 4.680 in untreated (diff +0.043, p=0.0769). Not significant marginally.

Mean pfs_months 4.645 in trastuzumab-treated vs 4.705 in untreated (diff -0.060, p=0.029). Direction opposite to predicted; likely reflects HER2+ being a poor-prognosis subgroup that receives trastuzumab.
Mean pfs_months 4.660 in olaparib-treated vs 4.696 in untreated (diff -0.036, p=0.33). Null marginally.
Mean pfs_months 4.686 in sacituzumab-govitecan-treated vs 4.694 in untreated (diff -0.007, p=0.85). Null marginally.
Mean pfs_months 4.630 in pembrolizumab-treated vs 4.704 in untreated (diff -0.074, p=0.018). Direction opposite to predicted; small effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stage IV disease (stage_iv=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than non-stage-IV.
Presence of brain metastases (has_brain_mets=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotonic fashion.
Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (clinical expectation: older patients tolerate therapy worse).
Node positivity (node_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.617 in stage IV vs 5.153 otherwise (diff -1.536, p<1e-300). Strongly supported.
Mean pfs_months 3.750 with brain mets vs 4.800 without (diff -1.050, p=1.4e-178). Strongly supported.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ ecog_ps; means by level: 0=>5.640, 1=>4.444, 2=>3.289
analyses.result_summary: PFS decreases monotonically with ECOG: 5.64 (ECOG 0) -> 4.44 (ECOG 1) -> 3.29 (ECOG 2). OLS slope ~ -1.17 mo per ECOG step. Strongly supported.

Pearson r = +0.7015 (p<1e-300). Direction is OPPOSITE the clinical expectation: older patients have substantially LONGER pfs_months in this dataset (~+0.18 months per year of age). Hypothesis as stated (older=shorter) is refuted; the empirical pattern is the inverse.
Mean pfs_months 4.681 (node+) vs 4.705 (node-), diff -0.024, p=0.29. Null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: ER positivity (er_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months.
PR positivity (pr_positive=1) is associated with longer pfs_months.
HER2 positivity (her2_positive=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than HER2 non-amplified.
HER2-low status (her2_low=1) is associated with longer pfs_months than HER2-zero.
PIK3CA mutation (pik3ca_mutation=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
BRCA1 or BRCA2 mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months on average.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ mean 4.865 vs ER- 4.297 (diff +0.568, p=3.7e-143). Strongly supported.
PR+ mean 4.824 vs PR- 4.462 (diff +0.362, p=5.8e-59). Strongly supported.
HER2+ mean 4.317 vs HER2- 4.776 (diff -0.459, p=6.1e-70). Strongly supported.
HER2-low mean 4.765 vs others 4.650 (diff +0.114, p=1e-6). Small but significant. Weakens after adjustment for ER.
PIK3CA-mut mean 4.328 vs WT 4.887 (diff -0.559, p=6.1e-144). Strongly supported.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind by brca1_mutation; stats.ttest_ind by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: BRCA1+: 4.669 vs 4.694 (diff -0.024, p=0.72). BRCA2+: 4.569 vs 4.696 (diff -0.127, p=0.08). Marginal/null, much smaller than other biomarkers; hypothesis weakly supported only for BRCA2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ki67_pct (proliferation index) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher serum albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months (better nutritional/inflammatory state).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months (cachexia / disease burden).
Higher LDH (ldh_u_l) is associated with shorter pfs_months (elevated tumor turnover).
Higher inflammation markers (crp_mg_l, nlr) are associated with shorter pfs_months.
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: r = -0.0914, p=3e-93. Direction supported; effect modest (~ -0.019 mo per % ki67 in OLS).
r = +0.1002, p=9e-112. Direction supported; OLS coef ~ +0.48 mo per g/dL after adjustment.
r = -0.1155, p=4e-148. Direction supported; OLS slope ~ -0.077 mo per % weight loss.
r = -0.0164, p=2e-4. Direction consistent but very small effect.
CRP r=-0.003 (p=0.46); NLR r=+0.003 (p=0.54). Null; inflammatory markers do not predict PFS in this cohort.
r = +0.0004, p=0.93. Null; tumor size is not marginally associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months specifically in ER-positive patients (treatment_tamoxifen × er_positive interaction is positive).
Tamoxifen effect on pfs_months is larger in postmenopausal patients than premenopausal patients.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient -0.058 (p=0.27). In ER+ subgroup, tamoxifen +0.023 (p=0.45); in ER-, tamoxifen +0.081 (p=0.03). No meaningful tamoxifen effect concentrated in ER+; refuted.
Interaction -0.043 (p=0.39). Postmenopausal stratum: tamoxifen effect not larger. Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in HER2-positive patients (treatment_trastuzumab × her2_positive interaction is positive).
Within HER2-positive patients, trastuzumab raises mean pfs_months versus untreated HER2-positive controls, after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, ER status, and other prognostic features.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction -0.028 (p=0.69). No HER2-specific trastuzumab benefit at the interaction level. Refuted.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_trastuzumab + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + albumin_g_dl + ki67_pct + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df[df.her2_positive==1]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: In HER2+ (n=9060), adjusted trastuzumab coefficient = -0.0004, p=0.95. No detectable benefit. Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib prolongs pfs_months specifically in BRCA1- or BRCA2-mutated patients (treatment_olaparib × (brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation) interaction positive).
After adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, biomarkers, and labs, olaparib has a positive effect on pfs_months in BRCA1/BRCA2-mutated patients.

analyses.code: stratified t-test in (brca1_mutation==1 | brca2_mutation==1) vs WT
analyses.result_summary: BRCA-mut: olaparib mean 4.933 vs control 4.587 (diff +0.346, p=0.041). BRCA-WT: -0.056 (p=0.14). Marginal positive signal in mutated subgroup.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib + age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + albumin_g_dl + ki67_pct + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df[(df.brca1_mutation==1)|(df.brca2_mutation==1)]).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib coefficient in BRCA-mut subgroup (n=2499) = +0.025, p=0.75. After full adjustment, signal vanishes — the marginal +0.35 was driven by unmeasured imbalance, not a true treatment effect. Refuted in adjusted analysis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in triple-negative (ER-/PR-/HER2-) breast cancer.
Sacituzumab govitecan prolongs pfs_months in TNBC.
Sacituzumab govitecan prolongs pfs_months in HER2-low patients.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by tnbc; multivariable regression in TNBC
analyses.result_summary: In TNBC (n=10496): pembro mean 4.366 vs ctrl 4.294 (diff +0.072, p=0.22). Adjusted coef +0.008, p=0.16. In non-TNBC: -0.114, p=0.002 (slightly worse). No TNBC-specific benefit; hypothesis refuted.

analyses.code: stratified t-test by tnbc; multivariable regression in TNBC
analyses.result_summary: In TNBC: sac-gov mean 4.228 vs ctrl 4.314 (diff -0.085, p=0.22). Adjusted coef +0.006, p=0.33. No effect. Refuted.
In HER2-low: sac-gov mean 4.737 vs ctrl 4.768 (diff -0.031, p=0.64). No benefit. Refuted.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib effect on pfs_months is concentrated in ER-positive patients (positive treatment_palbociclib × er_positive interaction).
Palbociclib effect on pfs_months is suppressed in HER2-positive patients (negative treatment_palbociclib × her2_positive interaction).

Palbociclib effect on pfs_months is suppressed in PIK3CA-mutated patients (negative treatment_palbociclib × pik3ca_mutation interaction; biological rationale: PI3K-pathway activation as CDK4/6-inhibitor resistance mechanism).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction +1.594 (p=9e-229). In ER+: palbo benefit ~+1.58 mo (p<1e-300). In ER-: palbo effect -0.017 (p=0.63). Strongly supported.
Interaction -1.364 (p=2e-118). In HER2-: palbo +1.350 (p<1e-300). In HER2+: palbo near zero. Strongly supported.
Interaction -1.623 (p=4e-255). In PIK3CA-WT: palbo +1.659 (p<1e-300). In PIK3CA-mut: palbo near zero. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Palbociclib has its largest effect on pfs_months when ER-positive AND HER2-negative AND PIK3CA-wildtype simultaneously, with negligible effect outside this triple-favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: df['favorable']=((df.er_positive==1)&(df.her2_positive==0)&(df.pik3ca_mutation==0)).astype(int); compare palbo-vs-control means within each level
analyses.result_summary: Triple-favorable (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT, 37.4% of cohort, n=18681): palbo mean 7.209 vs ctrl 4.300 (diff +2.908 months, p<1e-300). Outside triple-favorable (n=31319): palbo mean 4.319 vs ctrl 4.316 (diff +0.003, p=0.91). Effect concentrated almost exclusively in triple-favorable. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT subgroup, palbociclib effect on pfs_months is attenuated by higher ki67_pct (negative palbociclib × ki67 interaction).
In ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT patients with ki67_pct < 20%, palbociclib substantially prolongs pfs_months; in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT with ki67_pct ≥ 20%, palbociclib effect is absent.

analyses.result_summary: Within favorable subgroup, palbociclib × ki67_pct interaction = -0.135 (p~0). Each 1% rise in ki67 reduces palbociclib's PFS gain by 0.135 months. Strongly supported.

analyses.code: compare palbo-vs-control means in favorable subgroup, by ki67 quartile
analyses.result_summary: Quartile 1 (ki67 1.0-7.5%): palbo +4.93 mo (p~0). Quartile 2 (7.6-12.2%): +4.97 mo (p~0). Quartile 3 (12.3-19.6%): +1.72 mo (p=1e-83). Quartile 4 (19.7-100%): -0.05 mo (p=0.44). Strong monotone attenuation; hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment for age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, ER, HER2, HER2-low, PIK3CA, BRCA1/2, albumin, ki67, weight loss, and all six treatments, only palbociclib retains a positive PFS effect; tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab-govitecan, and pembrolizumab effects are null.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: palbociclib +1.102 (p<1e-300); tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.49); trastuzumab -0.010 (p=0.45); olaparib -0.026 (p=0.12); sacituzumab-govitecan +0.033 (p=0.052); pembrolizumab -0.020 (p=0.15). R²=0.79. Confirms only palbociclib has a clinically meaningful adjusted effect. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the multivariable model, age_years has a positive coefficient (older = longer PFS), opposite to the standard clinical expectation; this is a structural feature of the dataset rather than a confounder artifact.

Across the full cohort, the strongest negative prognostic features for pfs_months are stage_iv, ecog_ps, has_brain_mets, pik3ca_mutation, her2_positive, ki67_pct, and weight_loss_pct_6mo (all in the negative direction).
Across the full cohort, the strongest positive prognostic features for pfs_months are age_years, er_positive, and albumin_g_dl.

analyses.code: OLS with full adjustment, examine age_years coef
analyses.result_summary: age_years coefficient = +0.176 mo per year (p<1e-300, t=343). Mean PFS rises monotonically across age bins: 1.5 (40-50), 3.1 (50-60), 4.7 (60-70), 6.3 (70-80), 8.2 (80+). Robust to all adjustments. Direction confirmed (atypical for clinical reality).

Adjusted negative coefficients: ecog_ps -1.17, stage_iv -1.55, has_brain_mets -0.98, pik3ca_mutation -0.58, her2_positive -0.47, ki67_pct -0.019/%, weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.077/%. All p<1e-30. Supported.
Adjusted positive coefficients: age_years +0.176, albumin_g_dl +0.476, er_positive +0.541. All p<1e-30. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all six treatments and all 31 features, palbociclib is the only drug whose effect on pfs_months exhibits clinically meaningful heterogeneity (interaction |coef|>0.5, p<1e-10) — no other treatment shows substantial heterogeneity in any single-modifier interaction screen.

analyses.code: Exhaustive treatment × modifier OLS interaction screen across 6 treatments × 31 features (binary and continuous), sorted by interaction p-value.
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions (all palbociclib): pik3ca_mutation -1.62 p=4e-255; er_positive +1.59 p=9e-229; ki67_pct -0.052/% p=1e-174; her2_positive -1.36 p=2e-118; pr_positive +1.09 p=3e-117; her2_low +0.42 p=9e-19. After this, the next-strongest interaction (any treatment) is palbociclib × postmenopausal at p=0.004 (coef -0.13). No other treatment-modifier interaction has |coef|>0.5 and p<1e-10. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: The palbociclib pfs_months benefit is concentrated in patients who are simultaneously er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<20; in this refined subgroup palbociclib increases mean pfs_months by approximately +3.8 months versus untreated, while outside this subgroup the effect is essentially zero. Palbociclib is the only treatment in this dataset with usable subgroup heterogeneity; the other five treatments (tamoxifen, trastuzumab, olaparib, sacituzumab-govitecan, pembrolizumab) show no clinically meaningful subgroup-defined effect on pfs_months in any biomarker-defined or laboratory-defined subgroup.

analyses.code: df['fav_full']=((er_positive==1)&(her2_positive==0)&(pik3ca_mutation==0)&(ki67_pct<20)).astype(int); compare palbo-vs-ctrl in fav_full=1 and fav_full=0; refit with full adjustment.
analyses.result_summary: Refined favorable subgroup (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<20%, n=14174 = 28.3% of cohort): palbociclib mean 8.133 vs control 4.288 (unadjusted diff +3.845 months, p<1e-300). Adjusted (age, ECOG, stage IV, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, ki67) palbociclib coefficient = +3.842 mo (p<1e-300). Outside the refined subgroup (71.7%, n=35826): palbociclib -0.002 mo (p=0.94). The effect is fully concentrated within the four-feature subgroup definition, with each of the four predicates (ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA-WT, ki67<20%) materially required: removing the ki67 cutoff reduces the within-subgroup effect from +3.85 to +2.91; removing PIK3CA-WT from +2.91 to +1.92; removing HER2- from +1.92 to +1.58; removing ER+ leaves the effect at zero. No other treatment shows any subgroup of comparable magnitude on systematic search. Hypothesis strongly supported.

analyses.code: Final negative-control checks: stratified t-tests for tamoxifen (in ER+, ER+ postmeno, ER+ premeno); trastuzumab (in HER2+, HER2-low); olaparib (in BRCA1+, BRCA2+, BRCA1-or-BRCA2+, BRCA-WT); sac-gov (in TNBC, HER2-low, HR+/HER2-, HER2+); pembrolizumab (in TNBC, non-TNBC).
analyses.result_summary: All non-palbociclib treatment effects are < 0.4 months in magnitude in any clinically motivated subgroup, and all become null after multivariable adjustment (tamoxifen in ER+ adj coef +0.003 p=0.87; trastuzumab in HER2+ adj coef -0.0004 p=0.95; olaparib in BRCA-mut adj coef +0.025 p=0.75; sac-gov in TNBC adj coef +0.006 p=0.33; pembrolizumab in TNBC adj coef +0.008 p=0.16). No treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for these five drugs is supported.
